actions.detail: role 'PV Intake Specialist' bound to permission group
role 'PV Medical Reviewer' bound to permission group
role 'PV Safety Officer' bound to permission group
role 'PV Signal Analyst' bound to permission group
role 'PV Quality Auditor' bound to permission group

deferred.reason: PV-domain policies (no autonomous regulatory submission; conservative seriousness/expectedness when arguable; patient-identifier minimization in analyst-facing surfaces; late-case escalation) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route submission decisions exclusively to the Safety Officer (QPPV-equivalent).

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (Intake Validity Checker, Duplicate Detector, MedDRA Coding Assistant, Narrative Drafter, Signal Evaluation Copilot) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (safety databases: Oracle Argus, ArisGlobal LifeSphere, Veeva Vault Safety; intake channels: call center, medical information, E2B(R3) gateways, literature monitoring services; MedDRA dictionary subscription) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (E2B(R3) submission to FDA FAERS and EudraVigilance, case status write-back to the safety database, follow-up letter dispatch) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer gateway credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with Safety Officer approval — no autonomous regulatory submission, ever.

Golden eval sets (validity determination, seriousness classification, expectedness vs RSI, duplicate detection, expedited-vs-periodic routing) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Case/ICSR, AdverseEvent, Product, ActiveSubstance, Reporter, Patient, RegulatoryClock, ExpeditedReport, PeriodicReport, Signal, RSIVersion) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the pv_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (icsr_intake, medical_review, duplicate_review, signal_evaluation, late_case_investigation) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (case_priority_score, seriousness_classifier_v1) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest event anomaly + xgboost case-priority scorer) with GxP model-documentation expectations noted in grounding memories.
Display-label overlays ("ICSR", "SUSAR", "15-day clock", "Signal cluster", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
